title: Importance of elephants for dung beetle biodiversity and ecosystem functions
data_sources: Paired landscape observational surveys (elephant vs. livestock-dominated)

data_scope.treatments: elephant exclusion, smaller-ungulate exclusion, combined, control
data_scope.data_availability_statement: not confirmed in scraped metadata; article page returned HTTP 403

core_conclusion: Elephants are central hubs in the East African herbivore-dung beetle interaction network; their extinction would trigger disproportionate coextinction cascades in dung beetles. A 15-year exclosure experiment confirmed that excluding elephants depressed beetle abundance, diversity, and ecosystem functions far more than excluding smaller ungulates; observational data from livestock-dominated landscapes corroborated the result. Large, extinction-prone animals act as keystone resource-provisioners sustaining the biodiversity and ecosystem services of linked consumers.
why_it_matters: First long-term experimental proof of coextinction theory; quantifies the irreplaceable functional role of elephants for dung beetles beyond charisma; bridges network theory and conservation practice; warns about livestock-driven defaunation across African savannas.
why_nature: Resolves a long-standing theoretical debate with rare empirical evidence; integrates network modeling, 15-year experiment, and landscape observation into a triple-validated study; carries planetary conservation stakes for threatened megafauna; provides a transferable prediction-validation framework applicable to any hub species.
added_value_to_my_research: Graph neural network for coextinction prediction -> architecture transferable to spatial service-network fragility modeling
Spatial risk mapping of dung beetle loss using remote sensing -> mirrors GeoAI multi-source data fusion for accessibility surfaces
Ecosystem service deficit quantification (dung removal gap) -> 2SFCA-style supply-demand gap adapted to ecological services
Cross-biogeographic network generalization test -> analogous to spatial transferability / MGWR non-stationarity in my explainable ML work
Early-warning coextinction index using network centrality -> parallels spatial accessibility erosion monitoring for public health surveillance
